message:
feat(interpreter): add console.log/error/warn capture for deterministic replay (RC-1.10)

The baseline interpreter can now intercept console:log, console:error,
and console:warn hostcalls, capturing their output into a deterministic
ConsoleEntry log attached to the ExecutionResult. This enables:

- Deterministic replay: console output is recorded with the exact
  instruction index at which it was emitted, so replays produce
  identical logs
- Extension debugging: captured output surfaces in execution results
  without requiring external I/O
- Witness trail: each console call emits a HostcallDispatched witness
  event for the guardplane audit log

Implementation:
- Add ConsoleLevel enum (Log, Error, Warn) and ConsoleEntry struct with
  level, message, and instruction_index fields
- Add console_output: Vec<ConsoleEntry> to InterpreterCore state and
  ExecutionResult output
- Add dispatch_console_hostcall() routing console:log/error/warn
  capabilities to the capture buffer
- Add value_to_string() for JS-faithful stringification of all Value
  variants (handles -0→"0", NaN, Infinity, object placeholders)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
